findings.message: Unbounded query: `select(Item.name)` has no LIMIT, so it scans and materializes every row in the `items` table. The existing `get_items` endpoint deliberately caps reads at MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100 via `list_items()`; this endpoint bypasses that guard entirely. As the table grows the response time and memory allocation grow without bound, violating the bounded-read SLO established by the repository layer.
findings.suggestion: Add a LIMIT (e.g. `.limit(MAX_PAGE_SIZE)`) or accept `limit`/`offset` query parameters mirroring `get_items`, and import `MAX_PAGE_SIZE` from the repository. Example: `select(Item.name).order_by(Item.id).limit(MAX_PAGE_SIZE)`
